mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 5.25% represents a restrictive monetary policy stance that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS >= 4.0%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and reduced household income, which depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: If in the 3 months following UNRATE increases, S&P 500 delivers positive returns in more than 60% of cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: High levels of retail and food services sales indicate robust consumer demand, which supports corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS level and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is negative over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production signals contraction in manufacturing activity, which leads to lower corporate profits and negative equity market returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by 0.5% or more in one month
failure_condition: If after a monthly INDPRO decline of 0.5%+, the S&P 500 generates positive returns in more than 55% of instances over 20 days, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts and rising unemployment creates a double signal of economic contraction, reinforcing negative S&P 500 returns as both consumer confidence and construction activity decline.
failure_condition: If during joint HOUST<1400 and UNRATE>4.5% conditions, the 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE > 4%), further increases in unemployment are unambiguously negative for equities because they indicate demand destruction without the offsetting effect of potential monetary easing. Current UNRATE at 4.5% places us in this regime, so rising UNRATE should predict negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If in the high unemployment regime (UNRATE>4%) rising UNRATE leads to positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of occurrences, reject the regime-conditional hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains high and payroll employment declines, the economy faces a 'stagflationary' risk where monetary policy cannot stimulate without worsening inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If under the trigger condition the S&P 500 posts positive returns in more than 50% of 20-day windows, reject the interaction hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: High frequency housing starts data provides an immediate signal of economic activity. A strong housing starts reading relative to recent weeks (monthly data) leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investor sentiment improves.
failure_condition: If the correlation between HOUST level and 5-day forward returns is negative over any 2-year period, reject.
